articles.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
articles.description: Enjoy all the features, news, video analysis and color from around the World Cup as Brazil face Norway and England take on Mexico.

images.name: Mexico-England hotel [1296x729]
images.credit: James Manning/PA Images via Getty Images

images.name: Could beetroot juice be England's altitude remedy vs. Mexico?
images.caption: Could beetroot juice be England's altitude remedy vs. Mexico?

articles.headline: Mexico fans' serenata outside England hotel woke me up, and probably Three Lions too
articles.description: Playing in Mexico is intimidating, and it's more than the atmosphere of the Azteca. As England learned, it's Mexico's City's embrace of the cause.

images.name: England team hotel 260704 [1296x729]
images.caption: England team hotel
images.credit: Gerardo Luna / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Gomez: Mexico should be favorites vs. England
images.caption: Gomez: Mexico should be favorites vs. England

articles.headline: USMNT's Folarin Balogun has red card suspended by FIFA, available to face Belgium
articles.description: FIFA has suspended the red card U.S. striker Folarin Balogun received in the team's match against Bosnia-Herzegovina, making him available to face Belgium in the round of 16 at the World Cup.

images.name: us-goal.jpg photodesk [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Folarin Balogun has collected two Player of the Match awards this FIFA World Cup.
images.alt: Balogun yells in front of a blurred out audience background.

images.name: Balogun looks back on red card vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina
images.caption: Balogun looks back on red card vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina.

articles.headline: Every team's championship odds for the 2026 World Cup
articles.description: Odds for every team to advance through the round of 16 and to win the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Kylian Mbappé [576x324]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé continues to score goals and France continues to win.
images.alt: Kylian Mbappe #10 of France celebrates his goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 football match between France and Sweden.

images.name: Lionel Messi [608x342]
images.caption: Lionel Messi scored his record-extending 20th career World Cup goal in Argentina's win over Cabo Verde.

images.name: Mikel Oyarzabal [1296x729]
images.caption: Mikel Oyarzabal scored two goals for Spain in Thursday's win.

images.name: Tillman US celeb vs Bosnia [1296x729]
images.caption: Malik Tillman celebrates after scoring the United States' second goal against Bosnia-Herzegovina.
images.alt: Malik Tillman #17 of the United States celebrates scoring his team's second goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match between USA and Bosnia and Herzegovina at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium on July 01, 2026 in Santa Clara, California. (

images.name: Julio Enciso [1296x729]
images.caption: Julio Enciso and Paraguay are headed to the round of 16 after their win over Germany.

images.name: Cristiano Ronaldo [608x342]
images.caption: Cristiano Ronaldo netted a pair of goals in Portugal's rout of Uzbekistan on Tuesday.

images.name: Yamale for WC Odds file (260529) [1296x729]
images.caption: Spain's World Cup fortunes are tied to the health of Lamine Yamal's left leg.
images.alt: Spain's Lamine Yamal, right, scores his side third goal during the UEFA Nations League quarterfinal second leg match between the Netherlands and Spain at Mestalla stadium in Valencia, Spain, Sunday, March 23, 2025.

articles.headline: Mexico fans blast horns, set off fireworks outside England's hotel to try and disrupt players' sleep
articles.description: Despite police blockades, dozens of Mexican fans gathered outside the England national team's hotel until the early hours of Sunday morning, hoping to disrupt players' sleep ahead of their World Cup round-of-16 match against co-host Mexico.

images.name: England hotel [1296x729]
images.credit: Gerardo Luna / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Aguirre: Mexico will need an almost perfect match to beat England
images.caption: Aguirre: Mexico will need an almost perfect match to beat England

articles.headline: Mexico squad return luxury watches gifted by influencer
articles.description: Mexico have had to return luxury watches that were gifted to them by the YouTuber Stevewilldoit.

images.credit: Ryan Pierse - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images
